title: Neopallasia pectinata

intro: Neopallasia pectinata, also known as Korean angelica tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Korea and Eastern Asia, belonging to the family Araliaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, colds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Neopallasia pectinata include lignans, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Neopallasia pectinata is commonly prepared as a decoction or extract, and is also used in modern herbal formulations to promote health and well-being.
